name: Superheroes
description: Marvel, DC, and the iconic heroes and villains of comics

questions.q: What is the secret identity of Superman?

source.notes: Born Kal-El on Krypton; works as a reporter at the Daily Planet.

questions.q: What is the civilian name of Batman?

source.notes: Billionaire head of Wayne Enterprises.

questions.q: What is the civilian name of Spider-Man in his classic comic origin?

source.notes: High school student from Queens, New York.

questions.q: What is Wonder Woman's 'civilian' identity in comics?

source.notes: From the Amazon island of Themyscira.

questions.q: Billionaire Tony Stark is secretly which superhero?

source.notes: Built his first suit while a prisoner in Afghanistan.

questions.q: What is Captain America's real name?

source.notes: Given the 'super-soldier serum' during WWII.

questions.q: Bruce Banner transforms into which rage-fueled green superhero?

source.notes: Due to exposure to gamma radiation.

questions.q: What is the name of Batman's loyal butler at Wayne Manor?

source.notes: Raised Bruce after the deaths of his parents.

questions.q: In which fictional city does Batman fight crime?

source.notes: Based visually on New York and Chicago.

questions.q: What is the name of Thor's mighty, enchanted hammer?

source.notes: Can only be wielded by those deemed 'worthy'.

questions.q: In DC Comics, what fictional city is Superman's home?

source.notes: Often paired with Gotham as the 'sister city' in DC's East Coast.

questions.q: Wolverine is a member of which Marvel mutant team?

source.notes: Real name James 'Logan' Howlett.

questions.q: What is Green Lantern Hal Jordan's day job when he's not battling cosmic threats?

source.notes: Test pilot for Ferris Aircraft.

questions.q: What is the civilian name of The Flash in most DC Comics?

source.notes: Forensic scientist for Central City Police Department.

questions.q: Oliver Queen is the civilian identity of which emerald-clad DC hero?

source.notes: Billionaire vigilante of Star City, known for his Robin Hood style.

questions.q: Carol Danvers is the alter ego of which Marvel superhero?

source.notes: Formerly known as Ms. Marvel and Binary.

questions.q: Dr. Stephen Strange, the Marvel 'Sorcerer Supreme', was originally which kind of professional?

source.notes: A car crash damaged his hands, ending his surgery career.

questions.q: Scientist Hank Pym's iconic Marvel superhero alter-ego is what?

source.notes: Pym also took the identities Giant-Man and Yellowjacket over the years.

questions.q: What is Superman's name on his home planet, Krypton?

source.notes: Son of Jor-El and Lara Lor-Van.

questions.q: Spider-Man's classic pumpkin-bomb-throwing nemesis, Norman Osborn, goes by which villain name?

source.notes: First appeared in The Amazing Spider-Man #14 (1964).

questions.q: What fictional metal coats Wolverine's skeleton, making it virtually indestructible?

source.notes: Bonded to his skeleton via the Weapon X program.

questions.q: What is the name of Superman's home planet, which was destroyed shortly after he was sent to Earth?

source.notes: Its red sun is why Superman gets powers from our yellow sun.
